title: Zell
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Youmay|the [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassador]]|the [[Zhell/Legends|Zhell]] [[Species/Legends|species]]}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Zell.png]] |name=Zell |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=A [[Unidentified Majoor species|sentient species]][[Droids (1986) 3|''Droids'' (1986) 3]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |skin=Green |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children=[[Llez]] |affiliation=[[Republic Space Ranger]]s |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|No one threatens ''my son'' and gets away with it!|Ambassador Zell|}} [[Ambassador/Legends|Ambassador]] '''Zell''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of a [[Unidentified Majoor species|sentient species]] who served the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Majoor]], where he [[Parent/Legends|fathered]] a son, [[Llez]]. In [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]], Zell went on a [[Peace mission to Armath|peace mission]] to the planet [[Armath]], leaving Llez in the hands of two [[Droid/Legends|droids]], [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]. However, Llez got into trouble when he freed the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Reddjak]] from [[Prison/Legends|prison]], escaping to Reddjak's [[Blood Brother|ship]] and letting slip that his father was an ambassador and was headed for Armath. Eager to rob the convoy of [[Starship/Legends|starships]] that accompanied Zell, Reddjak launched [[Torpedo/Legends|torpedoes]] at the ambassador's ships, but Llez and the droids were able to self-destruct the [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] from Reddjak's vessel. Llez then contacted his father, who rushed over to the ship in order to save his son, and following a short fight with the pirate, he and his guards were able to overtake and arrest Reddjak and his [[Red Fury Brotherhood/Legends|crew]]. ==Biography== ===A mission of peace…=== {{Quote|Well, Scarlet Pirate.. what would your family think of your joining old ''Reddjak?''''"
"''There's only my dad..and he ''doesn't care'' much about me! He's only interested in running off on ''peace missions!''|Reddjak and Llez|}} [[File:ZellC-3PO-Droids3.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Zell threatening C-3PO]] Zell was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of a [[Unidentified Majoor species|sentient species]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Majoor]]. Formerly a member of the elite [[Republic Space Ranger]]s,{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-1|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} he came to be a wealthy [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassador]] for Majoor and [[Parent/Legends|fathered]] a son there, [[Llez]], the only family member with whom he lived. In [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]],{{DroidsComicDate}} Zell had an important [[Peace mission to Armath|peace mission]] on the planet [[Armath]], so he purchased the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] to be [[Teacher/Legends|tutors]] and companions for Llez in his absence. Before he left, C-3PO assured the ambassador that he would not regret acquiring the droids' services and noted that teaching was his specialty. As he departed, Zell threatened C-3PO that if his son got in any trouble at all, both droids would be sent to a scrap yard. He then bade goodbye to Llez and walked out. While his father was away, Llez, to the protests of his droid nannies, freed his hero, the ruthless [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Reddjak]], from [[Prison/Legends|prison]]. Not knowing Reddjak's violent tendencies and only knowing him to be a man of adventure, he accompanied the pirate to his [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the ''[[Blood Brother]]'', and told him of his father on the way, explaining that Zell did not care about him and was more concerned with peace missions. He further mentioned that his father was Ambassador Zell, whom Reddjak had heard of, and that he had left for a mission to Armath. Once they were back with Reddjak's [[Red Fury Brotherhood/Legends|crew]], the ship picked up a convoy of ships—Zell's peace convoy—which Reddjak ordered his men to attack so that he could rob them. Llez protested their innocence, but Reddjak did not care, ordering stowaways C-3PO and R2-D2, whom he thought were part of his crew, to take Llez away. ===…becomes quite violent=== {{Quote|I haven't had this much fun since my ''Space Ranger'' days!|Ambassador Zell|}} The trio hurried to the [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] room to try and prevent Zell's ships from being hit by [[Torpedo/Legends|torpedoes]], but since they were already launched, Artoo was forced to plug into the ship via [[Scomp link/Legends|scomp link]] and find the self-destruct code, which he activated just in [[Time/Legends|time]]—right as Zell and his crew realized that they had no way to escape. Llez then contacted his father's ship, informing Zell that he was on Reddjak's ship and begging him to run away from the danger. Zell, however, ordered his [[Unidentified starship captain|captain]] to prepare a security team to board the ''Blood Brother'' in order to save his son. The ambassador rushed in in the nick of [[Time/Legends|time]], for Reddjak had Llez by his neck. The ambassador ordered the pirate to let his son go, so Reddjak threw the child at his father's [[Limb/Legends|feet]]. [[File:WhackUnh-Droids3.jpg|thumb|right|280px|Zell whacks Reddjak.]] Zell comforted a distressed Llez but was then warned by C-3PO as Reddjak eagerly swung a [[Sword/Legends|sword]] at the ambassador's head. Zell was able to duck, however, and whacked the pirate in the [[Stomach/Legends|stomach]] and head in return. Reddjak begged for mercy as Zell shouted at him that was what he got for threatening his son, and he then had his guards take the pirate and his crew away. Observing Reddjak on the ground, he remarked how he had not had as much fun since his [[Standard day/Legends|days]] as a Space Ranger, which excited Llez, who had not known about his father's past. Zell then turned his attention to the droids, whom he held responsible for the day's trouble, but Llez then admitted that the whole ordeal was his fault and that Artoo and Threepio had actually saved them all. Zell instead thanked the droids and then walked away with Llez, promising that the two would go on missions together from then on and leaving C-3PO and R2-D2 masterless. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Get up, you ''coward!''|Ambassador Zell to Reddjak|}} Zell was originally not very active in his son's upbringing; Llez claimed that the words he knew his father by were "hello" and "goodbye." He threatened the droids with harsh punishment if his son got into any trouble during his absence. When his son was in danger, however, he spared no time in getting to him, angrily hitting Reddjak and calling him a coward for threatening Llez. The ambassador had fun defending his son from the pirate. After the incident, Llez was in awe of his father's actions in defense of him, which Zell was touched by, deciding to take an active role in raising the boy. He had [[Color/Legends|green]] skin and black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. ==Skills and abilities== {{Quote|I think you're ''great'', dad!|Llez, after witnessing his father thrash Reddjak|}} Zell, having been in the Space Rangers, was able to defeat Reddjak in combat and leave him on the ground, begging for mercy, which greatly impressed his son. ==Equipment== Zell carried a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and wore a [[Clothing/Legends|uniform]] that consisted of a yellow-and-purple [[Jumpsuit/Legends|jumpsuit]], blue shoes, a blue sash, a medal on his chest, and yellow shoulder pieces. He also wore a blue [[Helmet/Legends|helmet]] and used a gold-colored [[staff]]-like weapon to whack Reddjak. ==Behind the scenes== Zell first appeared in the [[Droids (1986) 3|third issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Droids (1986)|''Star Wars: Droids'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic series]], which was written by [[David Manak]], illustrated by [[John Romita Sr.]], and published by [[Marvel Comics]] on [[May 13]], [[1986]].{{BookCite|title=[[Marvel Age]]|number=[[W:c:marvel:Marvel Age Vol 1 40|Vol. 1 No. 40]]|publisher=[[Marvel Comics]] Group|date=[[July]] [[1986]]|issn=8750-4367}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Zell}} *[[Droids (1986) 3|''Droids'' (1986) 3]] {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *{{CSWECite|II||Llez}} *{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-1|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Ambassadors]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Members of the unidentified Majoor species]]
